briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech Engineering Handbook](https://w.pitula.me/fintech-engineering-handbook/)** — *Hacker News* — A comprehensive technical reference for engineers building fintech products, generating significant community interest.

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are set to migrate to a homegrown sovereign payments network, signalling a major shift away from US card giants.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A widely-shared resource cataloguing the darker consequences and risks of modern fintech products for consumers.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of Banking-as-a-Service middleware provider Synapse, raising urgent questions about fintech deposit protection.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Feds release proposed open banking regulations](https://betakit.com/feds-release-proposed-open-banking-regulations/)** — *BetaKit* — The Canadian federal government has published its long-awaited draft regulations for open banking, marking a pivotal step toward formally launching the framework.

- **[Float announces $85-million Series C round to support AI push](https://betakit.com/float-announces-85-million-series-c-round-to-support-ai-push/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech startup Float has raised an $85-million Series C to accelerate its AI-driven financial products for businesses.

- **[RBC fined $4.25M over incorrect credit card statements](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/rbc-fined-credit-card-statements-9.7249811?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's largest bank has been hit with a $4.25-million fine by regulators for sending customers inaccurate credit card statements.

- **[Why advocates say "inclusive succession" is a hidden market opportunity for Canada](https://betakit.com/why-advocates-say-inclusive-succession-is-a-hidden-market-opportunity-for-canada/)** — *BetaKit* — Advocates argue that helping underrepresented entrepreneurs acquire existing businesses represents a significant and largely untapped economic opportunity across Canada.

- **[Feds invest $3.2 million to support Manitoba's Black entrepreneurs](https://betakit.com/feds-invest-3-2-million-to-support-manitobas-black-entrepreneurs/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government is directing $3.2 million toward programs supporting Black-owned businesses in Manitoba, part of a broader push for inclusive economic growth.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Highly relevant background context as Canada's proposed open banking regulations drop this week — this piece traces the origins of Canada's push to move beyond screen scraping toward a secure, API-based open banking system that Atlantic fintechs and credit unions will need to navigate.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis of how open banking reshapes payments competition — essential reading for Atlantic Canadian fintechs and credit unions preparing for Canada's incoming regulatory framework.
